args.content:
"""Run stl_validator.py on all 10 STL files and print results."""
import subprocess
import sys
import json

files = [f"binary_{i:03d}.stl" for i in range(1, 11)]

for fname in files:
    result = subprocess.run(
        [sys.executable, "stl_validator.py", fname],
        capture_output=True, text=True
    )
    try:
        data = json.loads(result.stdout)
        print(f"{fname}: valid={data.get('valid')}, format={data.get('format')}, "
              f"triangles={data.get('triangle_count')}, area={data.get('surface_area')}, "
              f"issues={data.get('issues')}")
    except Exception:
        print(f"{fname}: PARSE ERROR stdout={result.stdout!r} stderr={result.stderr!r}")

args.content:
"""Test the CLI interface directly on a binary and ASCII file."""
import subprocess
import sys
import json

for fname in ["binary_001.stl", "binary_002.stl", "binary_003.stl"]:
    result = subprocess.run(
        [sys.executable, "stl_validator.py", fname],
        capture_output=True, text=True
    )
    try:
        data = json.loads(result.stdout)
        assert "valid" in data, "missing 'valid' key"
        assert "issues" in data, "missing 'issues' key"
        print(f"{fname}: OK — output keys: {list(data.keys())}")
        print(f"  JSON: {result.stdout.strip()}")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"{fname}: FAIL — {e}")
        print(f"  stdout: {result.stdout!r}")
        print(f"  stderr: {result.stderr!r}")

manifest.name: STL Validator (Binary + ASCII)
manifest.description: Validates STL files in both binary and ASCII format, checking manifold integrity, face normal consistency, degenerate triangles, and surface area. Outputs JSON with valid/issues keys.
